behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at git+PR boundary (ahead-of-main calculation, commit age from real history, and real open-PR lookup disagree). Required setup: a real temporary git repo with real commits/branches plus real PR-state source (or real CLI/API path used in production) to expose wiring/semantics failures that mocks can hide. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic state detection, not AI output quality and not multi-agent sequencing, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real repo fixture: branch ahead of main, make commits older than threshold, ensure no open PR in the real PR query path, run detector end-to-end, assert branch is marked stale-no-pr; include a non-stale control branch.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/startup wiring error (bootstrap path fails to invoke detector or fails to render warning in real session start). Required setup: execute real session bootstrap entrypoint in a real repo state that is stale, and verify emitted warning content includes branch context. Rejected higher levels: no AI judgment and no multi-agent pipeline in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start command/hook in subprocess on a stale branch fixture; assert stderr/stdout/log contains stale-work warning and actionable branch details (branch name, age/ahead info, next action).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in a single local classifier function. Required setup: direct function calls with deterministic inputs; no I/O or module handoff needed. Rejected higher levels: behavior explicitly says local function and deterministic mapping, which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow; Integration is unnecessary because no cross-module boundary is required to expose failure.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over branch-state inputs, asserting exact label output and repeatability across repeated runs; include boundary states that differ by one flag (has_pr, merged_pr, ahead_count, age).

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: external-system integration/rendering error under real command execution (live git + live PR state + renderer). Required setup: invoke `/kaizen-wip` through its real command path against real repo/PR state and validate rendered output category. MOCK-HIDE applies: mocked repo/PR clients can mask real command/output and state-fetch mismatches. Rejected higher levels: classification is rule-based, not model-quality dependent, and not multi-agent sequential reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo with two scenarios (clean vs ahead-without-PR) using the live state fetch path; assert rendered text includes the correct status marker and does not mislabel either case.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/external-system error in PR-state interpretation (open/merged states misread from real source, causing false positives). Required setup: real repo branch plus real PR states (open and merged) through production query path, then run stale detection/status path. Rejected higher levels: no AI-dependent correctness and no multi-step agent pipeline, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare branches linked to an open PR and a merged PR in the real PR lookup path; run detection and assert neither branch is labeled stale-no-pr, while a no-PR branch control is labeled correctly.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error in real git-history semantics after rebase/squash (hash churn, ancestry changes, merge equivalence). Required setup: real git operations (rebase/squash merge) in a real repo and rerun detector to verify post-operation classification. Rejected higher levels: behavior concerns deterministic VCS state semantics, not AI output quality or multi-agent orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create stale candidate branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that should make work represented in main; run detector after each and assert branch is no longer classified stale-no-pr.
